title: Project Wrap-Up & Failure Analysis
concept: Visualizing final detections and analyzing false positives or missed objects. 'Mini Project: Object Detector is complete!'.

exercises.prompt: What does mAP help you understand about your object detector's performance?

options.text: It shows how fast the detector runs.
It indicates the overall accuracy across classes.
It measures the total number of detections, regardless of correctness.

exercises.explain: mAP (mean Average Precision) helps summarize the accuracy of your object detector across all classes, showing how well it performs overall.

cards.title: Visualizing Detections
cards.body: Visualizing detections helps you see how well your object detector performs in real scenarios. It's like taking a snapshot of its work!

cards.title: False Positives and Missed Objects
cards.body: False positives are incorrect detections made by your model, whereas missed objects are things that your model failed to identify.

cards.title: Think of it Like a Treasure Hunt
cards.body: Imagine you are searching for treasure. False positives are false alarms—where you think you found treasure but it's just a rock. Missed objects are treasures you completely walked past!

cards.title: Example Visualization
cards.body: Consider an image with five bicycles. If your detector identifies seven bicycles, two of those detections are false positives. If it misses a bicycle, that's a missed object.

cards.title: Myth: More Detections = Better Performance
cards.body: It's a myth! Quality matters more than quantity. It’s better to have fewer accurate detections than many inaccurate ones.

cards.title: Detection Results Comparison
cards.body: Analyzing detection results can reveal strengths and weaknesses.

exercises.prompt: What should you analyze to improve your object detector's performance?

options.text: The number of tries made to train the detector.
The color of the objects detected.

exercises.explain: By analyzing false positives and missed objects, you can identify specific areas that need improvement in your object detector.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Visualizing detections does not help identify problems in an object detector.
exercises.explain: False. Visualizing detections is crucial for identifying issues like false positives and missed objects, helping to fine-tune detector performance.

exercises.prompt: In detection analysis, we look at __________ and missed objects to improve performance.
exercises.sentence: In detection analysis, we look at false positives and missed objects to improve performance.
exercises.explain: The correct term 'false positives' specifies the type of errors analyzed in performance evaluations.

exercises.prompt: Which statement correctly summarizes analyzing visualizations?

options.text: It helps you only find false negatives.
Visualizing helps track which objects were missed or wrongly detected.
It shows only the best detections without errors.

exercises.explain: The correct statement reflects how visualizing results can reveal both correctly detected and wrongly labeled objects, essential for fine-tuning.

exercises.prompt: What should you focus on while wrapping up your object detection project?

options.text: Just improving speed in your detections.
Evaluating false positives, missed objects, and refining detection results.
Completing it as fast as possible to move on.
Making the model look good rather than function well.

exercises.explain: Focusing on evaluating false positives, missed objects, and refining detection results is key to improving your model's performance and reliability. Mini Project: Object Detector is complete!
